pubmed_links: [Planas-Serra et al. (2025) Bi-allelic variants in the ribosomal protein RPS6KC1 cause a complex neurodevelopmental disorder](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41130203/)
summary: Bi-allelic variants in RPS6KC1 were identified in 13 individuals across 8 families, causing a neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by developmental delay, hypotonia, spastic paraplegia, and white matter abnormalities, with functional studies implicating disrupted mTOR/PI3K signaling and lipid metabolism. (novel candidate)
